title: Kaspersky Exposes New SilverFox APT Campaign Targeting Asia and Africa
summary: Kaspersky GReAT uncovered fresh SilverFox APT waves using new backdoors to steal data from companies in multiple sectors.
contents:
# Kaspersky Exposes New SilverFox APT Campaign Targeting Asia and Africa

## Overview
Kaspersky GReAT uncovered fresh SilverFox APT waves using new backdoors to steal data from companies in multiple sectors.

## Details
The campaign, active since December 2025, disguises malicious files as tax violation documents from official agencies. Attackers deploy a new Python-based ABCDoor backdoor via ValleyRAT, enabling file upload/download, screen streaming, clipboard access, and self-updates. A modified RustSL delivers ValleyRAT, first used late December 2025. Targets span industrial, consulting, trade, and transportation sectors. Anton Kargin of Kaspersky GReAT highlighted social engineering and multi-stage delivery minimizing detection.

## Location
India, Indonesia, South Africa, Russia

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @scpandura OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
APT, SilverFox, backdoor, ABCDoor, ValleyRAT, social-engineering, data-exfiltration, cyberespionage
